title: searsia marlothii
intro: Searsia marlothii, also known as the Natal currant or red currant, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Southern Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Searsia marlothii include treating fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, as well as being used as a treatment for wounds and sores. Searsia marlothii is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion for medicinal use, with the leaves and stems being used to make teas, infusions, and poultices.
